title: Sealed sender: cryptographic sender binding inside sealed payload
description:
The current sealed broadcast payload format is: sender_pub_id(64 bytes, ASCII) || mls_ciphertext. The sender_pub_id is self-asserted — any group member who holds the shared room HPKE secret key can forge the prefix to impersonate another member. The relay is still blind (relay blindness invariant holds), but intra-group sender authentication is not cryptographically enforced.

Fix: prepend an Ed25519 signature over the payload prefix inside the sealed envelope. The sealed plaintext becomes: sender_pub_id(64 bytes) || sig(64 bytes, Ed25519 over pub_id||mls_ct) || mls_ciphertext. Recipients verify the signature using the sender's Ed25519 verifying key (recoverable from pub_id via the relay's key directory or from MLS credentials).

This requires: (1) exposing the sender's verifying key in a lookup (GET_ED25519_KEY endpoint or reusing existing auth mechanism), (2) signing inside nie_core::hpke layer or in commands.rs before seal, (3) verifying on unseal in SEALED_DELIVER handler, (4) updating the E2E test to verify signature check.

Why: The shared MLS export_secret approach provides relay blindness (correct) but not per-sender authentication. A malicious group member can claim to be any other member in the sealed payload. This is documented in a code comment at the SEALED_DELIVER handler.
close_reason: Already implemented: current code uses MLS process_incoming for sender authentication (cryptographically bound). No self-asserted pub_id prefix exists in the current sealed payload format. See commands.rs lines 1185-1186, 2369.
